message:
chore(beads): close 3 orphaned in_progress beads, mark r73v blocked on corpus prep

Orphans (no live owner after the prior swarm was killed):
* zk9q: HGETALL field-order divergence — already canonicalized in
  live_oracle_hgetall_field_order_matches; live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime
  passes 137/137 against vendored oracle.
* w579: CLIENT INFO/ID/HELLO drift + wording — already shipped in
  160b9e5/8b0dfb9/deec7b8/865eb1f; live_redis_core_client_matches_runtime
  passes 124/124.
* s5su: RDB stream encoding — sub-tasks hjub/qi6z/6zk9 (decoders + type-21
  encoder) all closed; live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime 167/167;
  DUMP/RESTORE round-trip green. File-level corpus harness deferred to
  br-frankenredis-ian4.

r73v moved to blocked, dependency added on br-frankenredis-8w1e (the
20-fixture upstream RDB corpus harvest step we can ship before the load+
re-emit and --loadrdb cross-impl gates can run). Reasoning comment added
to r73v noting sub-task (d) REDIS0011 header is already test-covered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Spawn vendored redis-server, populate keys covering every type tag fr-persist emits (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family — see RDB_TYPE_* in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h), trigger SAVE, capture dump.rdb.  {"id":"frankenredis-8xmq","title":"Downgrade FEATURE_PARITY.md replication status to honest assessment","description":"# Honest Replication Status

## What
Change FEATURE_PARITY.md "Replication baseline | parity_green" to "parity_gap" or "in_progress" until cross-process replication actually works.

## Why
The current "parity_green" rating for replication is dishonest. The state machine logic works in isolation but no bytes flow between processes. Honesty about project status is a prerequisite for good engineering.

## Implementation
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: change replication row status to "in_progress"
2. Update notes to honestly describe what works (FSM logic, PSYNC decision math, offset tracking) and what does not (actual TCP replication)
3. After replication epic is complete, upgrade back to parity_green with evidence

## No Dependencies
This should be done immediately, before any replication implementation work.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:17.272701785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:52:37.119459043Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:52:37.119284593Z","close_reason":"Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md replication row from parity_green to in_progress with honest assessment of what works (TCP replication, PSYNC, FULLRESYNC) and what does not (runtime REPLICAOF command, replica-of-replica chains, FR-to-FR E2E, diskless replication)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-ian4","title":"[HIGH] s5su follow-up: 20-fixture upstream stream RDB corpus + --loadrdb round-trip gate + structured fuzzer","description":"Parent br-frankenredis-s5su shipped at command + DUMP/RESTORE level (RDB type 15/19/21 decode + type 21 encode; live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime 167/167; live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream green). The file-level acceptance criteria (20 upstream-produced .rdb fixtures × decode + re-encode + 'redis-server --loadrdb' XINFO STREAM FULL identity; structure-aware fuzzer for fuzz_rdb_structured.rs) remain open. Steps: (1) Build the fixture corpus by spawning vendored redis-server, populating each shape (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time), running BGSAVE, capturing dump.rdb. (2) Add fr-conformance harness that loops the corpus through fr-persist::decode_rdb + re-emit + cross-load. (3) Extend fuzz_rdb_structured.rs with arbitrary::Arbitrary stream generator, gate end-to-end upstream loadback behind REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:41:04.230661384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:41:04.230661384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-icsm","title":"EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking","description":"# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic. The benchmark harness must exist before any optimization work can be proven.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:22.979891472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057699153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057479858Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: benchmark harness (bxlo), baselines (gess), regression gate (1zxx) all closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1zxx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.512824074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-bxlo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.348066219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gess","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.429198141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ie3f","title":"redis parity: CONFIG GET/SET/RESETSTAT/REWRITE should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks CONFIG GET, CONFIG SET, CONFIG RESETSTAT, and CONFIG REWRITE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/config-get.json, config-set.json, config-resetstat.json, and config-rewrite.json, but config_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:10363 serves all four subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection while preserving existing arity and subcommand validation order.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:07:16.387435544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:13:26.624349627Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:13:26.622405788Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-r48","title":"Fix panicking unwrap in CONFIG SET parameter lookup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-19T04:08:48.974371759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T04:09:46.983794195Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T04:09:46.983002218Z","close_reason":"Fixed: replaced unwrap() with expect() with descriptive message at CONFIG SET parameter lookup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r71v","title":"[HIGH] Reality-check vs upstream Redis 7+: CLUSTER FORGET/COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, ACL GETUSER selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID ENTRIESADDED","description":"Run a focused parity audit against vendored redis-server (7.2.4) for the Redis 7-era surface that hasn't been live-oracle exercised yet. Specifically:

(a) CLUSTER subcommands beyond MYID/SHARDS/SLOTS/INFO: FORGET, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, RESET, BUMPEPOCH, FLUSHSLOTS, ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE, LINKS. Verify error wording when cluster mode is disabled and arity errors against upstream's exact phrasing.
(b) ACL GETUSER selectors field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the field is structurally present in our reply but the channel/key/command vectors per selector may not match upstream when the user has zero selectors vs N>0 selectors.
(c) XADD MINID and LIMIT options (Redis 7.0+): we accept them but their effect on stream trimming + the +OK/(nil)/error reply shape needs upstream verification.
(d) XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID arguments (Redis 7.2+).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: spawn vendored redis-server, send each combination via the existing live-oracle harness, surface every divergence as a follow-up [HIGH] bead. File one bead per family that fails.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","close_reason":"All 4 slices shipped: (a) CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS handler + cluster-disabled wording wired; FORGET/REPLICATE/REPLICAS already wired with upstream wording. (b) ACL GETUSER selectors[] returns empty array matching upstream when no extra selectors exist. (c) XADD MINID/LIMIT validation per t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply. (d) XSETID ENTRIESADDED + MAXDELETEDID validation + no-such-key wording per t_stream.c::xsetidCommand.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acl","cluster","reality-check","redis7","streams"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r72v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 protocol parity: HELLO 3 push-frame routing + map/set/double/big-number types in real handlers","description":"Our parser learned RESP3 reply types via ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (br-frankenredis-ozcx), but the runtime handlers haven't been audited end-to-end for the RESP3-only reply shapes:

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:50:23.174897054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:50:23.173045710Z","close_reason":"Completed in fc3d541","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hello","protocol","push","resp3"]}
-{"id":"frankenredis-r73v","title":"[HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

The DUMP/RESTORE round-trip we just landed (br-frankenredis-v8s5) is at the wire level for single keys; this bead is the FILE level for full snapshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aof","persistence","rdb","roundtrip"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r73v","title":"[HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

The DUMP/RESTORE round-trip we just landed (br-frankenredis-v8s5) is at the wire level for single keys; this bead is the FILE level for full snapshots.","status":"blocked","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:43:03.483418489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aof","persistence","rdb","roundtrip"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-r73v","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-8w1e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:42:33.787270608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"frankenredis-r73v","author":"ubuntu","text":"Blocked on br-frankenredis-8w1e (corpus harvest). The 4-step acceptance (decode every upstream RDB type tag cleanly + re-emit byte-stable + LZF-encoded save loadable upstream + REDIS0011 header verified) cannot proceed without the 20-fixture corpus. Sub-task (d) RDB version header is already verified by existing tests in fr-persist (fr-persist/src/lib.rs:3439, 3450, 3460 assert REDIS0011); sub-tasks (a)/(b)/(c) need 8w1e first. Will lift to open when 8w1e lands.","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:43:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r74v","title":"[HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks","description":"(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: drive scenarios from a multi-client harness (extends fr_p2c_006 replication journey infrastructure).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","close_reason":"Sharded pub/sub (SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH/SUNSUBSCRIBE/CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS/SHARDNUMSUB) already wired. Keyspace notification event-name parity for BITOP/PFADD/*STORE/XGROUP/blocking-pop family shipped. Multi-client NUMSUB/NUMPAT cluster scenarios deferred to substantive cluster work (separate bead).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["keyspace-notifications","pubsub","scalability"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r75v","title":"[HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface","description":"Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_scripting.json + core_function.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","close_reason":"Major slices shipped: (c) EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement covered by existing tests. (g) Redis 7+ #!lua flags=no-writes shebang now honoured by eval_cmd/evalsha_cmd. (e) redis.* surface (call/pcall/error_reply/status_reply/sha1hex/log/replicate_commands/breakpoint/debug/set_repl/KEYS/ARGV) all wired. (a) EVAL multi-key/arg + return-coercion covered. (b) EVALSHA cache + SCRIPT EXISTS/LOAD/FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC covered. (f) MULTI inside EVAL rejected. (d) FUNCTION LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE binary format tracked under r83v.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["eval","function","lua","scripting"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r80v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 push frame routing: pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, tracking redirects must use '>' framing on RESP3 sessions","description":"After HELLO 3, upstream Redis switches asynchronous-server-message paths from RESP2 Array to RESP3 Push frame ('>' prefix per https://github.com/redis/redis-specifications/blob/master/protocol/RESP3.md#push-type). Today our runtime continues to emit Arrays:

(a) PUBSUB messages: when a SUBSCRIBE'd client receives a PUBLISH, the ["message", channel, payload] reply must be a Push, not an Array. Same for ["smessage", ...] (sharded), ["pmessage", pattern, channel, payload], ["subscribe"|"unsubscribe"|"psubscribe"|"punsubscribe"|"ssubscribe"|"sunsubscribe", channel, count].

(b) Client-side caching invalidations: with CLIENT TRACKING ON in RESP3 mode, the server pushes ["invalidate", [keys...]] frames to the tracking redirect target on key mutation. Today our runtime queues these on the wrong stream and as Array.

(c) Server-side notifications (when more land): keyspace notifications via CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events route through the same pub/sub layer; verify the push framing follows.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend the live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 on the dedicated connection used for these cases, then assert the wire frame on the receiving side starts with the '>' byte under RESP3 (and '*' under RESP2).

Scope guard: this bead only covers the framing of asynchronous-server-message paths. Synchronous request/reply RESP3 promotions (HGETALL→Map, SMEMBERS→Set, ZSCORE→Double) are tracked separately under br-frankenredis-r72v.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:15:56.813244354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T05:15:56.811371439Z","close_reason":"Completed in 5c83cec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client-tracking","pubsub","push","resp3"]}

-{"id":"frankenredis-s5su","title":"HIGH: RDB type 15 (stream) encoding diverges from upstream — file interchange broken","description":"## Summary

Our `fr-persist` serializes streams under RDB type byte 15 (our historical assignment), but upstream uses a different layout under the same tag plus a family of additional tags for consumer group state. Project memory note `project_rdb_stream_incompatibility.md`: \"RDB type 15 stream encoding differs from Redis, breaking file interchange\".

Impact: any user who takes an upstream `dump.rdb` and boots our server on it gets a parse failure or (worse) silently-truncated stream state. Symmetrically, our RDB cannot be loaded by upstream. That violates AGENTS.md Compatibility Doctrine strict mode, which promises \"maximize observable compatibility for V1-scoped APIs\" with \"no behavior-altering repairs\".

## Surface to verify

Upstream `rdb.h` / `rdb.c` (see `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h` and `rdb.c`):

- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS` (15) — listpack-bodied stream
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2` (19) — adds per-consumer seen_time
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` (21) — adds per-consumer active_time (7.2+)

Each shape writes:
1. radix tree: (stream-id, listpack)
2. stream metadata: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added
3. per consumer group: name, last_id, PEL size, PEL entries, consumer list
4. per consumer: name, seen_time (v2+), active_time (v3+), pending entry IDs

Our encoder should write v3 by default (matches upstream 7.2+) and decode all three.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip):**
- Fixture corpus: 20 upstream-produced `.rdb` files with stream shapes covering each axis (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time).
- Round-trip gate:
  1. `fr-persist::rdb::decode(bytes)` must succeed on every fixture.
  2. Re-encode; feed to upstream `redis-server --loadrdb`, query `XINFO STREAM FULL`; canonicalize and compare to our canonicalized `XINFO STREAM FULL` post-load.
  3. Reverse: encode our in-memory stream, have upstream load it, compare stream state vector.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, structure-aware):**
- Extend the existing `fuzz_rdb_structured.rs` with a stream-producing `arbitrary::Arbitrary` generator: random entries, groups, PELs. Invariant: for any generated shape, `decode(encode(x)) == x` AND upstream can load the encoded bytes (this last check runs gated, driven by a `REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1` env var).
- Extend `fuzz_rdb_decoder.rs` with upstream-produced corpus (minimized from the 20 fixtures above) so crashes on adversarial streams are found quickly.

## Related

- Frames our broader persistence story for streams; the `core_stream.json` conformance fixtures already exist (`crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json`, 114 KB).
- Does not depend on (but complements) frankenredis-rv89 (hash field TTL) and frankenredis-0zyf (fr-protocol conformance). All three share the upstream-fixture harvesting infra.

## Acceptance

- 20/20 upstream fixtures decode cleanly.
- Round-trip through upstream yields `XINFO STREAM FULL` identity for every fixture.
- Fuzz target logs zero crashes / zero mismatches over 1M iterations.

## Labels

fr-persist, rdb, streams, conformance-gap, file-interchange, testing-conformance-harnesses","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T03:30:14.504545661Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:13:53.323992378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance-gap","file-interchange","fr-persist","rdb","streams","testing-conformance-harnesses"]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-w1m","title":"Wire CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold (lua-time-limit) to runtime","description":"## Problem

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs line 1294 defines `const COMMAND_TIME_BUDGET_MS: u128 = 5000`. This matches Redis's busy-reply-threshold default (5000ms) but is not configurable.

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold <milliseconds> controls when Redis starts rejecting commands with BUSY errors during long-running scripts or operations. CONFIG SET lua-time-limit is the legacy name.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add command_time_budget_ms to ServerState (initialized to 5000)
2. Handle CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold and lua-time-limit (both set the same value)
3. Replace the const usage with the runtime field

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Add field, CONFIG SET handler, replace const","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:16.267291289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:13.058081750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:13.056821354Z","close_reason":"busy-reply-threshold/lua-time-limit wired: ServerState.command_time_budget_ms + CONFIG SET/GET + replaces const","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-w1m","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:19.891477646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-zjii","title":"Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines","description":"# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:08.439128211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.319272656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.318401347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
